acceptance_targets: [chapter_16_award_001] PASS if the revised clause-reveal/refusal sequence at snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_16.md:127, snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_16.md:163, and snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_16.md:181 includes at least one concrete aboveground consequence anchored to a named person or bodily image before Owen withdraws, and no more than one consecutive paragraph of abstract term-definition follows the clause's first appearance. FAIL if the beat is still carried primarily by semantic explanation.

[chapter_16_craft_001] PASS if the revised discovery span at snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_16.md:121, snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_16.md:123, snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_16.md:135, and snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_16.md:153 explicitly answers all three plausibility questions on the page: why the clause becomes readable now, why nobody has already used it, and what immediate limitation or collateral effect keeps it from reading as a free plot handout. FAIL if Owen still reaches full confidence in a single clean interpretive pass without any local obstacle or limit.

[chapter_16_prose_001] PASS if, across the revised target beats at snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_16.md:123, snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_16.md:131, snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_16.md:181, snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_16.md:193, and snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_16.md:203, no more than two corrective-negation constructions of the forms `It was not X. It was Y.`, `X did not mean Y. It meant Z.`, or `He had not ...` remain, and at least one clause meaning is conveyed through physical consequence rather than definitional contrast. FAIL if three or more such constructions still carry those beats.

[FA-001] Pass if a cold read of snapshots/cycle_01/FINAL_NOVEL.md:4948-5006 can point to both stakes at once: the planetary breach risk and at least one specific upstairs life-or-death consequence Owen is refusing to prevent in that moment. Fail if the scene still relies mainly on generalized siege language and abstract pressure to carry the refusal.

[FA-010] Pass if snapshots/cycle_01/FINAL_NOVEL.md:4818-4819 still makes the battle's pressure bodily immediate without using the established teeth/jaw shorthand. Fail if the revised line remains in the same oral-pressure register.

findings.acceptance_test: PASS if the revised clause-reveal/refusal sequence at snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_16.md:127, snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_16.md:163, and snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_16.md:181 includes at least one concrete aboveground consequence anchored to a named person or bodily image before Owen withdraws, and no more than one consecutive paragraph of abstract term-definition follows the clause's first appearance. FAIL if the beat is still carried primarily by semantic explanation.
findings.problem: The chapter's largest turn lands first as semantic explanation rather than as an unforgettable human choice. Once the hostile-wand clause appears, the prose spends multiple beats defining terms and asserting certainty, so the refusal reads as an elegant mechanism before it reads as a devastating irreversible act. That keeps the set piece below shortlist-level force.
findings.rewrite_direction: At snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_16.md:127, snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_16.md:131, snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_16.md:163, and snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_16.md:181, compress the legal-style gloss and spend that space on one or two concrete imagined consequences aboveground tied to named people or bodily outcomes. Keep Owen's analytic frame, but make the instant of possibility and the instant of withdrawal register as human cost before the narration returns to abstraction.

findings.acceptance_test: PASS if the revised discovery span at snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_16.md:121, snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_16.md:123, snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_16.md:135, and snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_16.md:153 explicitly answers all three plausibility questions on the page: why the clause becomes readable now, why nobody has already used it, and what immediate limitation or collateral effect keeps it from reading as a free plot handout. FAIL if Owen still reaches full confidence in a single clean interpretive pass without any local obstacle or limit.
findings.problem: The combat-ending clause becomes legible and trustworthy too cleanly for a hinge this consequential. The chapter does explain why the branch surfaces now, but Owen moves from noticing marginal bars to full confidence in the reading and its battle-scale effect in one smooth interpretive pass, which makes the discovery feel partly handed to him at the exact moment the plot needs it.
findings.rewrite_direction: In snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_16.md:121, snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_16.md:123, snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_16.md:135, and snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_16.md:153, add one genuine obstacle to legibility before certainty locks in: a failed inference, a partial reading, or an explicit limitation/collateral effect Owen must reason through. Keep the clause, but show why it stayed dormant, why nobody else has used it, and what it would not solve, instead of letting the chapter jump straight from sighting to mastery.

findings.acceptance_test: PASS if, across the revised target beats at snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_16.md:123, snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_16.md:131, snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_16.md:181, snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_16.md:193, and snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_16.md:203, no more than two corrective-negation constructions of the forms `It was not X. It was Y.`, `X did not mean Y. It meant Z.`, or `He had not ...` remain, and at least one clause meaning is conveyed through physical consequence rather than definitional contrast. FAIL if three or more such constructions still carry those beats.
findings.problem: The chapter overuses corrective-negation sentence templates: `It was not ...`, `X did not mean ... It meant ...`, `He had not ...`. By the time Owen reaches the refusal, the prose is repeatedly parsing terms in the same shape instead of varying rhythm and pressure, which turns a strong conceptual voice into a detectable tic.
findings.rewrite_direction: At snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_16.md:123, snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_16.md:131, snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_16.md:141, snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_16.md:181, snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_16.md:193, and snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_16.md:203, keep at most one or two deliberate corrective-negation turns and convert the rest into different sentence shapes or physical/consequential beats. Let some meaning arrive through image, pressure, or action instead of repeated definition-by-negation.

findings.acceptance_test: Pass if a cold read of snapshots/cycle_01/FINAL_NOVEL.md:4948-5006 can point to both stakes at once: the planetary breach risk and at least one specific upstairs life-or-death consequence Owen is refusing to prevent in that moment. Fail if the scene still relies mainly on generalized siege language and abstract pressure to carry the refusal.
findings.problem: The chapter discovers the hostile-wandwork clause with admirable clarity, but the decisive refusal at snapshots/cycle_01/FINAL_NOVEL.md:4948-5006 is still staged mainly as technical calculus against generalized battle pressure. Named people cross Owen's mind, but the human counterweight remains mediated through abstraction, screams, and system-language, so the choice reads more as solved theorem than as an unbearable live refusal.
findings.rewrite_direction: Revise snapshots/cycle_01/FINAL_NOVEL.md:4948-5006 so the house-clause decision is interrupted by at least one concrete upstairs consequence tied to an identifiable human stake Owen already knows: a recognizable voice, a specific casualty report from the flue, a named command failing, or another local signal that is more than generic battle noise. Keep the cosmic breach logic intact, but make Owen choose against something immediate and human in the same scene rather than only against the category of 'the battle above.'

findings.acceptance_test: Pass if snapshots/cycle_01/FINAL_NOVEL.md:4818-4819 still makes the battle's pressure bodily immediate without using the established teeth/jaw shorthand. Fail if the revised line remains in the same oral-pressure register.
findings.problem: Recurring somatic-vocabulary pattern: the prose keeps returning old-work strain to teeth/molars/jaw across 7 chapters, and this chapter's 'pressure arrived in his teeth' at snapshots/cycle_01/FINAL_NOVEL.md:4818 is no longer carrying fresh information. In the battle-under-undercroft opening, the smoke, soot, and wall-impact already provide stronger local signals.
findings.rewrite_direction: Replace the teeth-based sensation in snapshots/cycle_01/FINAL_NOVEL.md:4818-4819 with a different sensory route that belongs to this specific opening: smoke in the lungs, impact through the wall and shoulder, split-nail pain, or burn-pulse in the palm. Avoid teeth/molars/jaw language in that span.

non_negotiables: Maintain heading contract exactly (# Chapter N).
Elliptical clipping is not permitted in dialogue or narration.
Avoid transcript cadence in high-stakes dialogue.
Avoid stiff over-formality in dialogue; use contractions and colloquial rhythm where character-true.
Preserve productive dialogue roughness; do not clean living speech into balanced thesis statements.
Reduce procedural drift when it displaces embodied consequence.
Maintain character-specific voice signatures from style bible.
Honor aesthetic risk policy: sanitization is the primary craft risk — do not soften, euphemize, or retreat from the story's established register. When in doubt, err toward rawness.

aesthetic_risk_policy.creative_risk_policy: Lean into the premise's sharpest contradiction: school-year comedy, adolescent awkwardness, punitive cruelty, and cosmic dread may coexist in the same novel without canceling each other. When a scene offers a cleaner version and a truer version, choose the truer one unless the character is actively refusing perception on the page.
aesthetic_risk_policy.euphemism_penalty: If pain, cruelty, fear, or moral ugliness would leave a bodily or environmental trace, render that trace instead of summarizing it into tasteful haze.

dialogue_rules.default_contraction_use: High. Contractions are the spoken norm for most characters; fuller forms are reserved for Snape, ritual language, or deliberate emphasis.

prose_style_profile.chapter_texture_variance: Alternate scene density, dialogue load, and movement mode aggressively: confrontation against quiet meals, subterranean revelation against corridor farce, ritual compression against spring drift, so no consecutive chapters feel built from the same emotional or structural template.
prose_style_profile.diction: British boarding-school realism under wartime pressure, with speculative vocabulary introduced only when Owen's attention earns it.
prose_style_profile.narrative_distance: Close third limited through Owen, with only brief sensory widenings that he could plausibly notice in the moment.
prose_style_profile.rhythm_target: Alternate analytic accumulation with blunt corporeal sentences. Quiet chapters should breathe; punishment and ritual chapters should tighten without clipping grammar.
